probe_layers.object_probe: family basis and concept offset should map to partially shared but region-parameterized cortical patterns
probe_layers.attribute_probe: attribute axes should map to locally reusable feature gradients rather than isolated neurons only
probe_layers.relation_probe: bridge-role lifts should map to cross-area coordination patterns rather than only local codes
probe_layers.stress_probe: novelty and retention stress should map to changes in plasticity-sensitive versus stable subspaces

mathematical_meaning.core_statement: Brain-side mapping does not need to start from whole-brain reconstruction. It can start from inventory-conditioned probe families.
mathematical_meaning.formation_answer: If inventory is the right abstract object, then brain probes should preserve family patch structure, local concept offsets, and local attribute gradients.
mathematical_meaning.runtime_answer: Brain activity should reflect region-parameterized realizations of the same inventory-conditioned encoding system rather than unrelated codes per area.

verdict.core_answer: The inventory can now serve as the abstract probe source for brain-side mapping and falsification.
verdict.next_theory_target: compose object, attribute, relation, and stress probes into a concrete P4-ready brain-side bundle
